name: Black Rice
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the beans in 1 quart of cold water overnight. Drain and rinse.
2. Place 3 cups of broth and the beans in a stockpot, and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 2 to 2 1/2 hours, or until tender. Drain the beans and reserve the broth and beans. Set aside.
3. Place 1 1/2 cups of the bean broth in a small saucepan (if needed, add water to make 1 1/2 cups). Place over low heat to keep warm.
4. In a stockpot over high heat, heat a couple of tablespoons of broth. Add the onion and garlic, and sauté until the onion is softened, about 5 minutes. Add the rice and stir constantly for 1 minute. Add the wine and cook for 2 minutes. Add the tomatoe and cook for 2 minutes.
5. Gradually add the bean broth, 1/2 cup at a time, stirring until the liquid is absorbed before adding more broth. This will take 20 to 25 minutes. Add the beans and remaining broth. Season with cumin, cayenne, salt, and pepper to taste. Remove from heat and stir in cilantro; serve immediately.
